title: angelica archangelica f. tschimganica
intro: Angelica archangelica f. tschimganica, also known as Tschimganica angelica, is a subspecies of the angelica plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce pain and prevent infections. The plant's health benefits include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil for use in traditional medicine, and its roots and leaves are used in herbal preparations to promote overall well-being.
